Lumberjack's Cap  
 


Slot: HEAD
AC: 10
STA: +3
WT: 1.5 Size: TINY
Class: ALL except NEC WIZ MAG ENC
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)
Lumberjack's Cap

Slot: HEAD
AC: 10
STA: +3
WT: 1.5 Size: TINY
Class: ALL except NEC WIZ MAG ENC
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Item Type:Armor
Appearance:Leather
Tint:
 
Color (RGB):0, 0, 0
Stackable:No
Merchant Value:18 pp 7 gp 5 sp 0 cp
Tribute:20
Lucy Entry By:Kerasota
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-09-11 08:14:22
Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Expansion: Ruins of Kunark
Average Price: No Data Pricing Data...
Rarity: Common
Level to Attain: 25
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

NPC Name
a forest giant ancient
a forest giant arbor
a forest giant verdant

NPC Name
a mountain giant craig
a mountain giant steep

Frontier Mountains
NPC Name
a mountain giant brae
a mountain giant hillock
a mountain giant patriarch
a mountain giant tump
mountain giant prospector

Warsliks Woods
NPC Name
a forest giant
a forest giant evergreen
a forest giant greenwood
a forest giant sapling

Burning Woods

Warsliks Woods

Drop

# Dec 03 2004 at 6:50 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1623630129087
Scholar
404 posts
FYI, a fellow guildie was soloing the giant fort in Burning Woods last night, so I went to help him out. I got this, plus the Tump Stump for the Dark Oak Shellelah from one of the giants within 5 kills. How do I know that? Because after the 5th, we got swarmed and my buddie died, with this poor wolfie running pellmell to the zone line with 2 giants and an sarnak on my tail.

Edited, Fri Dec 3 06:50:51 2004

Don't know if anyone ever reads this post, but I have NEVER seen a Lumberjack's Cap drop in WW ever, and I have levelled four characters here from mid 20's to 29. Also asked my buds who all have iksar mains, nobody has ever seen one drop from anything in WW...first place you'll see these is off hillies in Frontier Mountains...

uhhhh the forest loop is WAY more rare then this cap... not just my own experince either. you should be overjoyed you have seen the earing 3 times... over the cap. and the weaponsa re easy... they are in their hands.

Magic Headgear
QuoteReply
# Mar 22 2002 at 9:46 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592251924332
Scholar
303 posts
It's good to get a magic item for your head inventory slot, especially if you're of the Wrestler class and need a magic headbutt attack for ghouls and such. BONK!
▲ Page top
RE: can't wear hat
QuoteReply
# Dec 04 2001 at 10:22 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592758578638
189 posts
Well it's probably just a bug...I had a bug that when ever i got in water a big clear mace looking thing poped up on me , and the same with a Mithril helm i was putting it on and it wouldn't go on me so i sold it and kept my coif ^_^
Tazain 17 ranger
▲ Page top
RE: can't wear hat
QuoteReply
# Jan 25 2003 at 1:20 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591750863733
36 posts
Your "bigt clear mace looking thing" is not a bug. It is a "watcher signal torch" dropped off goblin watchers. when equipped, this ever helpful device changes in the big clear mace, signaling to you that you are in fact in the water. Players not knowing they submerged is a wide spread problem, and lord konws were we would be without this amazing torch.
▲ Page top
Can't wear hat
QuoteReply
# Nov 29 2001 at 2:41 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592758706368
View User Forum
16 posts
I am a 25 iksar monk and the hat will not work on my char. I took off everything and it still will not go on. Anyone know why this is. I see from previous posts, other iksar have used this item.

#scruples, Posted: Jul 29 2001 at 7:06 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) i have one for sale on the tholuxe paells server for 40pp, send me a tell the to scrupl

#Anonymous, Posted: Jul 10 2001 at 6:51 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) LOL man i got one but i have to get it over to my monk hehe
I noticed...
QuoteReply
# Jun 19 2001 at 3:40 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592248835747
9 posts
I noticed a 4hp increase when I equip this cap too ... is that based on the +3 STA?
▲ Page top
RE: I noticed...
QuoteReply
# Aug 05 2001 at 1:39 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591763039499
13 posts
HP is directly effected by STA, depending on you class of corse, for Casters its about 1 STA = 1.2 HP, for Hybrids(rangers, bards, etc..) its about 1 STA = 1.7-1.9(not sure), and for Fighters it seems about 1 STA = 2.9-3.1(not sure again). If you have the time go on and see, I dont ATM and when i do i will fix this to be exact.
▲ Page top
RE: I noticed...
QuoteReply
# Oct 07 2002 at 1:25 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593362109465
10 posts
The stamina is a level effect. If you wear a +3 stamina item as a level 1 character, you will probably get around ... 3-4 hit points. If you get +3 stamina as a level 40 character, you will get about 7-8 hit points. I have an alt, when I wear crystacean breastplate (+15 stamina) I get around 50 hit points. Its level depended. But im not sure if its class depended too, it probably is.

Sullon Zek:

Wracx Thunderlizard -- Level 34 shaman.
Alparslan -- Level 41 Shadowknight.
▲ Page top

__DEL__1591761628721
17 posts

but a lvl 10 can't have a last name

#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 03 2001 at 2:27 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I have this on erollisi marr if anyone is interested , send tell to Zaxar
looks
QuoteReply
# May 17 2001 at 5:28 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592248826941
3 posts
When you put this on it looks like a polor bears head. Its a nice item.
▲ Page top
RE: looks
QuoteReply
# Aug 21 2001 at 2:13 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591765525919
Scholar
748 posts
Its appearance depends on race; it has the same graphic as a leather or patchwork skullcap. Looks like a bear head on barbarians, like a cattle skull on iksar, etc. Whatever a leather skullcap would look like on your character's race, this will look identical to that.
▲ Page top
RE: looks
QuoteReply
# May 31 2001 at 6:54 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591760632651
13 posts
It's a leather look...looks like a polar bear head on barbarians only.
▲ Page top
I like my hat but...
QuoteReply
# May 15 2001 at 8:18 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Hello;

I may sound like a complete newbie here, but my AC increases by 15 when I wear this hat. Could this be because of the increase to my STA?
▲ Page top
RE: I like my hat but...
QuoteReply
# Jun 03 2001 at 3:44 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591754966424
Sage
119 posts
It's because item AC does not translate directly to actual AC benefit.
I've read that you get 1.55 actual AC per 1 item AC, at least under one particular set of circumstances; I haven't checked to see if it holds for everyone.
Seems to work here though; 10*1.55 = 15.5. Then either this would be truncated to 15 at some point, or the final AC would be (and the original decimal part would be less than 0.5), either way increasing the integer value of AC by 15.
Anyway, I should do more experimenting so I can give a more definite answer next time. I see this question asked a lot.
▲ Page top
RE: I like my hat but...
QuoteReply
# Jul 17 2001 at 1:09 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591755813047
Scholar
20 posts
It is not 1.55ac per 1 ac on armor it is all dependent on what your defence skill is. If your defence is 0 the ac is 1-1. I don't know the translation but the 1.55 is wrong.
▲ Page top
RE: I like my hat but...
QuoteReply
# Jul 17 2001 at 3:26 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591754055236
Sage
140 posts
It's dependant on your defense skill, but also your agility. While agility mostly determines chances of a defensive skill happening, and therefore the speed at which that skill is learned, it also affects directly how much actual AC is added for every labeled AC.
▲ Page top

Might help if you said which server you were looking for it on.
